title: DSDS API Guideline
description: Documents the code-level interface of a component — its configurable properties, emitted events, content slots, CSS custom properties, CSS shadow parts, data attributes, and public methods. When a component supports multiple platforms, each platform gets its own API guideline with the `platform` property identifying which implementation it describes.

apiProperty.description: A configurable property of a component. Pairs a human-readable type string with an optional machine-readable JSON Schema for tooling.

name.description: The property name as used in code (e.g., 'variant', 'size', 'disabled', 'onClick').

type.description: A human-readable type description for display (e.g., "'primary' | 'secondary' | 'ghost'", 'boolean', 'IconComponent').

schema.description: A JSON Schema (Draft 2020-12) object defining the property's type in a machine-readable form. Follows the same conventions as OpenAPI parameter schemas.

description.description: What this property controls, how it affects the component, and any constraints or side effects.

required.description: Whether this property must be provided by the consumer.

defaultValue.description: The default value in its native JSON type. Accepts any JSON type: string (e.g., 'primary'), number (e.g., 44), boolean (e.g., true), object, array, or null.

since.description: The version in which this property was introduced.

deprecationNotice.description: What to use instead, if deprecated.

apiEvent.description: An event emitted by a component.

description.description: When this event fires, what it communicates, and how consumers should respond.

payload.description: Description of the event payload shape (e.g., '(event: MouseEvent) => void', '{ value: string, index: number }').

since.description: The version in which this event was introduced.

apiSlot.description: A content insertion point of a component.

name.description: The slot name. Use 'default' for the unnamed/default slot.

description.description: What content this slot accepts and any constraints on its children.

acceptedContent.description: A description of the allowed content (e.g., 'Plain text or a text node. Do not nest interactive elements.').

since.description: The version in which this slot was introduced.

apiCssCustomProperty.description: A CSS custom property exposed by a component for external styling.

name.description: The full property name including the -- prefix (e.g., '--button-background', '--button-border-radius').

description.description: What visual attribute this property controls.

defaultValue.description: The default value of the CSS custom property (e.g., 'var(--color-action-primary)', '1rem', 'transparent').

since.description: The version in which this custom property was introduced.

apiCssPart.description: A CSS shadow part exposed by a web component for external styling via ::part() selectors.

name.description: The part name as used in ::part() selectors.

description.description: What element this part exposes and what styling it enables.

since.description: The version in which this part was introduced.

apiDataAttribute.description: A data attribute used by a component to reflect state or variant information into the DOM.

name.description: The full attribute name including the data- prefix (e.g., 'data-state', 'data-variant').

description.description: What this attribute reflects and how it can be used for styling or testing.

since.description: The version in which this attribute was introduced.

apiMethod.description: A public method on a component instance.

description.description: What this method does, when to call it, and any side effects.

parameters.description: Description of the parameter signature (e.g., '(options?: { preventScroll: boolean }) => void').

returnType.description: Description of the return value (e.g., 'void', 'Promise<void>', 'HTMLElement | null').

since.description: The version in which this method was introduced.

api.description: Documents the code-level interface of a component on a single platform. Lists the configurable properties, emitted events, content slots, CSS hooks, data attributes, and public methods that consumers interact with. For multi-platform components (e.g., React + Web Component + Vue), create one API guideline per platform and use the `platform` property to distinguish them.

type.description: Identifies this guideline as an API specification.

platform.description: The platform or framework this API describes. Use when a component has different APIs across platforms (e.g., 'react', 'web-component', 'vue', 'angular', 'ios', 'android'). When omitted, the API is assumed to be the single/default platform.

properties.description: Configurable properties (props, attributes, inputs) that consumers pass to the component.

events.description: Events emitted by the component that consumers can listen to.

slots.description: Content insertion points where consumers can project child content.

cssCustomProperties.description: CSS custom properties exposed for external styling.

cssParts.description: CSS shadow parts exposed for external styling via ::part() selectors.

dataAttributes.description: Data attributes that reflect component state or configuration into the DOM.

methods.description: Public methods available on the component instance.
